page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an AMD-based instance belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 24 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration provides 24 physical cores with a single thread per core on a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates strong performance characteristics. Its single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core performance falls within the top 25%. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% tier and memory bandwidth tests placing in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models and strong text generation speeds for medium 7B LLM models. Standard operations like database tasks, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput perform in the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and bundled 2400 GB NVMe SSD provide a dense resource profile suitable for memory-intensive workloads without requiring dedicated GPU hardware. However, a notable tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce performance variability compared to dedicated CPU instances under sustained, heavy multi-core loads.

This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale caching layers, database hosting, and moderate-sized language model inference tasks. The combination of 192.0 GB RAM and fast NVMe storage also makes it appropriate for data processing pipelines, backend web serving, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa-powered instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 24 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core). Built on the x86_64 architecture with a shared CPU allocation, it includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside strong multi-core and LLM text generation capabilities. While database and web serving operations perform at average levels, the 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio offers a resource-dense profile for memory-bound workloads. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, and medium-scale LLM inference.

tagline: A memory-optimized cloud instance featuring AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 24 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs on x86_64 architecture with shared CPU allocation
192.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Strong text generation speeds for small and medium LLM models
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and data processing
